 {"id":"ft-6piwt","title":"[P0 BLOCKER] frankenterm-core lib has 61 compile errors under --no-default-features — blocks all --no-default-features test/bench runs","description":"## Symptom
`cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features` fails with 61 errors. Panes 3 and 4 in the swarm both hit this while trying to verify their respective commits.

## Representative error
```
error[E0599]: no method named `record_event_with_cx` found for this struct
  --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:6157
  help: there is a method `record_event` with a similar name

error[E0599]: no method named `get_text_with_cx` found for struct `std::sync::Arc<(dyn WeztermInterface + 'static)>`
  --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs:3272:43
  note: `wezterm::PaneTextSource` defines an item `get_text_with_cx`, perhaps you need to implement it
```

## Root cause hypothesis
Asupersync migration landed `_with_cx` **callers** in some files without landing `_with_cx` **implementations** on the corresponding traits/structs, specifically under the default feature set (where the legacy impls are gated off but the new impls aren't gated on).

## Scope
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs (record_event_with_cx)
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs (get_text_with_cx + PaneTextSource trait alignment)
- 61 errors total; likely same pattern elsewhere

## Fix direction
1. Run `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features 2>&1 | grep 'error\[\|error:' > /tmp/errors.txt` to enumerate all 61.
2. Group by method name; for each missing `*_with_cx` method:
   - If the non-cx variant exists, add a default impl or route `with_cx` through it (respects cancel semantics).
   - If neither exists, revert the caller to the non-cx variant with a TODO referencing ft-xbnl0.2 epic.
3. Verify with `cargo check` clean, then `cargo test --lib --no-default-features`.

## Priority
P0 — blocks swarm from running any tests under --no-default-features, which is the recommended lean test surface.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:29:56.725238Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:50:22.680762Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:50:22.680537Z","close_reason":"Added no-feature cx compatibility shims and ungated fallback *_with_cx adapters so frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features compiles again","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-6tr7c","title":"web_framework.rs:103 cargo test blocked by mixed fastmcp_rust/asupersync Cx and TcpListener types","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:34:57.848420Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:12:26.893700Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:12:26.893518Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-761tz","title":"[HIGH] disk_pressure NaN sample silently reports Green tier instead of failing closed","description":"# Defect

## Concrete failure path

1. Caller passes `DiskSample { usage_fraction: NaN, .. }` to `DiskPressureMonitor::update_with_sample`. Easy trigger: `available/total` when `total == 0` (e.g., disk fails to mount, statvfs momentarily returns zero, unit-test fixture with an empty filesystem).
2. `EwmaEstimator::update` calls `sample.clamp(0.0, 1.0)`. **`NaN.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` returns NaN** — this is documented Rust behaviour but caller-hostile. The clamp does not sanitise the NaN.
3. `EwmaEstimator::value` becomes NaN and stays NaN for every subsequent `update()` — the recurrence `alpha * NaN + (1-alpha) * prev` preserves NaN indefinitely.
4. `PidController::update` receives `pid_error = NaN - target`. Its internal integral + derivative go NaN and the output is NaN. Permanent until `reset()`.
5. `classify_tier(NaN, thresholds)` compares `NaN >= thresholds.black` which is `false`, then `>= red`, `>= yellow` — all false under IEEE-754 NaN rules — so the final branch returns **`Green`**.
6. Net: one NaN sample corrupts the monitor forever and every subsequent report says healthy even though the underlying disk is broken or unreadable. Operators trusting the Green tier take no action.

## Severity rationale

- Silent wrong answer, not a crash or a visible error.
- Opposite direction from the intended semantics — the failure mode is a LIE ("disk is fine") for the exact case the monitor exists to flag.
- One bad sample is sticky: the monitor cannot self-recover.

## Fix sketch

1. `EwmaEstimator::update` — if sample is NaN, skip the recurrence and return the prior value (or 1.0 if uninitialised). Do not poison internal state.
2. `PidController::update` — if error is NaN, skip state update and return 0.0 (or last output). Same no-poison rule.
3. `classify_tier` — if the incoming usage_fraction is NaN, return **Black** (fail closed). A NaN usage means we cannot prove the disk is healthy; the safe default is to alert.
4. Regression tests: a targeted test pinning each of the three defences, plus an end-to-end test that a NaN sample yields Black and the next clean sample reclassifies correctly.

## Scope

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/disk_pressure.rs` only. No cross-crate API changes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:39:31.506608Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:14.150284Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:14.149967Z","close_reason":"Landed in c5a02edb (fix(disk_pressure): fail closed on NaN in Ewma, PID, and classifier) + regression tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-76d9i","title":"[audit] storage/search: validate_fts_query doubles full-scan cost on lexical queries","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:33:26.455763Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:34:43.790999Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3610,"issue_id":"ft-76d9i","author":"jemanuel","text":"Perf/contention finding from 2026-04-23 storage audit: every lexical FTS search currently does a redundant preflight full scan before the real query. Evidence: validate_fts_query runs SELECT COUNT(*) FROM output_segments_fts WHERE output_segments_fts MATCH ?1 LIMIT 1 at storage.rs:15010-15016, and search_fts_with_snippets calls it unconditionally before preparing/executing the actual snippet/BM25 query at storage.rs:15042-15048. COUNT(*) with LIMIT 1 still has to enumerate all matches for broad FTS terms, so common queries pay for two MATCH walks and hold the read connection longer under load. Expected: syntax validation should be a cheap prepare/step of the real query shape or a SELECT rowid ... LIMIT 1 probe, not an aggregate count over the whole match set.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:34:43Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-7awje","title":"Align robot_types envelope serialization with CLI omit-null contract","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:33:35.810723Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:41:55.614794Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:41:55.614462Z","close_reason":"Already landed concurrently in 8fdfd114 (robot_types omit-null envelope serialization + regression tests).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-7axpo","title":"protocol_recovery deterministic jitter defeats thundering-herd protection","description":"# [HIGH] protocol_recovery: fake jitter

**Subsystem:** protocol_recovery / retry
**File:** crates/frankenterm-core/src/protocol_recovery.rs (`RecoveryConfig::delay_for_attempt`)

## The hole

\`\`\`rust
let jitter_seed = ((attempt as f64 * 7.13).sin().abs()).mul_add(2.0, -1.0);
let jittered_ms = jitter_range.mul_add(jitter_seed, capped_ms).max(1.0);
\`\`\`

`sin(attempt * 7.13)` is fully deterministic. Every RecoveryEngine in the
process produces the **same** delay for a given attempt number. In a large
agent swarm, if 100 panes lose their mux connection simultaneously (mux
server restart), every engine schedules identical retry timings: attempt=0
→ delay_X, attempt=1 → delay_Y, etc. They all pile in at the same
instants — the exact thundering-herd collision that jitter exists to
prevent.

Pre-fix: `jitter_fraction: 0.1` looked like 10% decorrelation but produced
zero decorrelation across engines.

## Fix

Replaced the deterministic `sin()`-derived seed with
`rand::rng().random_range(-jitter_range..=jitter_range)` — same approach
already used in `retry.rs::RetryPolicy::delay_for_attempt`. Consistent with
how the other module in the same crate spells jitter.

Kept the `jitter_range > 0.0` fast-path so `jitter_fraction == 0.0` is
still bit-exact deterministic (tests rely on this).

## Caller impact

`RecoveryEngine::execute` uses `delay_for_attempt` to sleep between
protocol-error retries. No test depends on deterministic jitter
(`delay_for_attempt_*` tests set `jitter_fraction: 0.0`), so the fix is
drop-in.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:26:26.938808Z","created_by":"p3-review-audit","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:43:28.102548Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:43:28.102198Z","close_reason":"Fix already shipped in fdd1e817; regression tests added in bf4a63b9 (delay_for_attempt_jitter_is_randomized_across_calls + delay_for_attempt_zero_jitter_is_deterministic).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["protocol-recovery","reliability","retry"]}

 {"id":"ft-trby3","title":"[reality-gap] RecorderBackendKind::FrankenSqlite is enum-reachable but runtime-unreachable","description":"# Claim (README)

README.md:62 lists "**Replay & Forensics**: Capture, replay, and
diff decision graphs for post-incident analysis and regression
testing" as a core feature. No distinction between backends is
offered — the user's mental model is "replay just works."

# Reality (code)

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/recorder_storage.rs:560-578`:

```rust
match config.backend {
    RecorderBackendKind::AppendLog => { ...opens storage... }
    RecorderBackendKind::FrankenSqlite => Err(
        RecorderStorageError::BackendUnavailable {
            backend: RecorderBackendKind::FrankenSqlite,
            message: "frankensqlite backend not yet implemented".to_string(),
        }
    ),
}
```

The enum variant is public, serde-deserializable (confirmed by
presence on `RecorderStorageConfig.backend` field), and reachable
from any operator who writes `backend = "frankensqlite"` (or
whatever serde rename applies) in their ft.toml's recorder section.

# Failure shape

Operator configures frankensqlite → app bootstraps → recorder
bootstrap returns `BackendUnavailable` → app either:
  (a) aborts with the "not yet implemented" message (informative);
  (b) silently falls back to AppendLog (if caller does);
  (c) propagates the error and the whole watch daemon fails to start.

Which branch fires depends on callers of `bootstrap_recorder_storage`
— worth verifying in a follow-up. Best case (a) wastes operator's
time; worst case (c) is a surprise crash.

# Recommended disposition

Either:
  (a) Remove `FrankenSqlite` from the serde-exposed enum until the
      backend is actually implemented. Zero blast radius — the
      variant is only reachable by operator opt-in today.
  (b) Keep the variant but add a deprecation/preview flag that
      operators must explicitly set (e.g., `experimental_backend =
      true`) so accidental selection is impossible.
  (c) Implement the FrankenSqlite backend.

(a) or (b) ship in minutes; (c) is a subsystem-sized undertaking.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:44:30.571102Z","created_by":"reality-check","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:55.998924Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:51:20.514877Z","close_reason":"README + flight-recorder docs now disclose that append_log is the only shipped recorder backend and frankensqlite remains rollout/test-only until bootstrap support lands.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["reality-gap","recorder","storage"]}
 {"id":"ft-trot7","title":"telemetry::Histogram::record poisons total_sum/min/max on f64::INFINITY (NaN guard is too narrow)","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/telemetry.rs:339\` — \`Histogram::record\`
guards against NaN but does **not** guard against \`f64::INFINITY\` or
\`f64::NEG_INFINITY\`. The recently-added [ft-b4l62] guard catches NaN
because NaN poisons \`partial_cmp\` and the running sum, but Inf has the
same poisoning shape and slips through unflagged.

## Concrete failure path

1. Caller invokes \`Histogram::record(f64::INFINITY)\`. Easy upstream
   trigger: any latency calculation of the form
   \`numerator / denom_seconds\` where \`denom_seconds == 0.0\` (clock
   skew, mocked time fixture, division-before-clamp bug). Or a
   subtraction of two timestamps that overflowed to Inf in a longer
   pipeline.
2. Line 340 \`if value.is_nan() { return; }\` — Inf is not NaN, falls
   through to mutate state.
3. Line 344 \`self.total_sum += value\`. \`f64 + INF = INF\`. \`total_sum\`
   is now \`INF\` permanently — every subsequent finite sample preserves
   the INF (\`INF + finite = INF\`).
4. Line 348-349 \`if value > self.max { self.max = value }\`. Initial
   \`max = NEG_INFINITY\` so \`INF > NEG_INFINITY\` is true → \`max = INF\`.
   Every later finite value compares \`finite > INF\` → false, so \`max\`
   stays at INF forever.
5. \`Histogram::mean()\` (line 401) returns \`INF / count = INF\`.
6. \`Histogram::summary()\` returns \`Some(INF)\` for \`mean\`, \`max\`, and
   potentially \`p99\`. \`HistogramSummary\` is \`Serialize\`; serde_json's
   default serializer emits \`null\` for non-finite f64 (RFC 8259 forbids
   Inf in JSON), silently dropping the value from telemetry payloads
   that downstream dashboards consume.
7. Symmetric for \`record(f64::NEG_INFINITY)\` — \`total_sum\` and \`min\`
   become NEG_INFINITY permanently, mean reports \`-INF\`.

## Severity rationale

- Same poisoning shape as ft-b4l62 (NaN), ft-761tz (NaN in
  disk_pressure), ft-yskcu (NaN in continuous_backpressure), ft-7s0vw
  (overflow in event_id) — silent wrong answer with no self-recovery.
- Existing NaN guard makes the absence of an Inf guard surprising:
  reviewers reading the code see \`is_nan()\` and assume the histogram
  is hardened, when it is not.
- Reachable via legitimate division-by-zero in any client computing
  durations or rates as f64.

## Fix sketch

1. \`Histogram::record\` (line 340) — broaden the guard from
   \`if value.is_nan()\` to \`if !value.is_finite()\`. \`f64::is_finite\`
   is true iff the value is neither NaN nor ±Infinity.
2. Update the doc comment and \`[ft-b4l62]\` reference to note that the
   guard now covers Inf as well.
3. Regression tests covering: (a) record(INFINITY) is no-op,
   total_sum stays 0.0, mean stays None; (b) record(NEG_INFINITY) is
   no-op; (c) record(finite) then record(INFINITY) then record(finite)
   — second finite blends correctly without Inf contamination;
   (d) summary().max stays Some(finite_value) after an Inf attempt.

## Scope

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/telemetry.rs\` only. No cross-crate API
changes. \`HistogramSummary\` shape unchanged.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:58:02.402142Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:14.413298Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:14.413032Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit f73e139a: Histogram::record now uses !value.is_finite() to reject both NaN and ±INFINITY. 4 regression tests added covering pos-Inf, neg-Inf, all-Inf, and summary().max stays finite after Inf attempt. All 74 histogram-related tests pass against /tmp/ft-rv-p0-target. Pattern matches the arithmetic-poison family alongside ft-b4l62 (NaN guard, original), ft-761tz, ft-yskcu, ft-7s0vw.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-twnmf","title":"[MEDIUM] br-43bbl WebGPU readback polling rewrite is untested at the changed timeout/callback boundary","description":"Commit 6f41130c rewrote WebGpuTexture::read() from a single wgpu::PollType::Wait call to a manual Poll loop with 10ms recv_timeout slices (crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:163-193). That changes the control flow around map_async completion, timeout expiry, and callback disconnect handling, but the test module in the same file still only covers helper routines like padded_readback_bytes_per_row and copy_padded_readback_to_image (crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:738-1003) and never exercises read() itself. This means a regression in the new bounded-poll behavior can land without any automated signal. Add a focused regression harness or extracted helper test that proves the success, timeout, and disconnected-callback cases for the new loop.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:54:02.292808Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:05.990393Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:05.990145Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-tzwuw","title":"mcp_tools ca.search/ca.view/ca.status accept timeout_secs=0, fire immediate-timeout","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs\` cass tools (ca.search line
590, ca.view line 683, ca.status line 763) all pass
\`params.timeout_secs\` directly to \`cass_client_with_timeout()\` without
validating that it's >= 1.

The JSON-Schema declarations for these tools advertise
\`"timeout_secs": { "type": "integer", "minimum": 1, "maximum": 600,
"default": 15 }\` (lines 526, 636, 726), but \`serde_json::from_value\`
does not enforce JSON-Schema bounds — it happily deserializes \`0\` as
a valid u64.

## Concrete failure path

1. MCP client (or any caller that doesn't pre-validate against the
   schema) sends \`{"query": "x", "timeout_secs": 0}\` to ca.search.
2. \`serde_json::from_value\` accepts the 0 → \`params.timeout_secs = 0\`.
3. \`cass_client_with_timeout(0)\` constructs a CassClient with
   \`timeout = Duration::from_secs(0)\` (cass.rs:781-784).
4. \`run_with_cx\` (cass.rs:961) calls
   \`timeout_with_cx(cx, Duration::from_secs(0), cmd.output()).await\`
   — a zero-duration timeout fires immediately, before \`cmd.output()\`
   has any chance to execute.
5. Returns \`CassError::Timeout { timeout_secs: 0 }\` to the MCP client
   on EVERY call. The client sees a confusing "cass timeout (0 secs)"
   error and may not realize the issue is its own \`timeout_secs: 0\`
   parameter rather than a slow cass binary.

Same defect shape as ft-t62hq (already fixed for wa.wait_for and
wa.send tools at lines 1149, 1873) — three sibling cass tools were
missed in that sweep.

## Severity rationale

- Silent UX failure: caller's request always times out without ever
  invoking the underlying tool.
- Reachable via any client that doesn't validate against the
  advertised schema (in practice: most language SDKs that just pass
  through user-supplied JSON, plus any test fixture that omits
  timeout_secs default-checking).
- Not a crash, not data corruption.

## Fix sketch

Mirror the ft-t62hq guard at the top of each \`call(\)\` for the three
cass tools:

\`\`\`rust
if params.timeout_secs == 0 {
    let envelope = McpEnvelope::<()>::error(
        MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS,
        "timeout_secs must be >= 1 (got 0)".to_string(),
        Some(
            "The ca.<tool> schema declares timeout_secs with \\
             minimum: 1; omit the field to use the default (15)."
                .to_string(),
        ),
        elapsed_ms(start),
    );
    return envelope_to_content(envelope);
}
\`\`\`

## Regression tests

Three integration-style tests at the params layer:
- \`ca_search_rejects_zero_timeout_secs\`
- \`ca_view_rejects_zero_timeout_secs\`
- \`ca_status_rejects_zero_timeout_secs\`

Each invokes the tool with \`timeout_secs: 0\` and asserts the response
is an Error envelope with \`code = MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS\` and the
"timeout_secs must be >= 1" message — proving the guard fires before
\`cass_client_with_timeout\` is ever called.

## Scope

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs\` only. No schema changes —
the schemas already declare \`minimum: 1\`. No changes to \`cass.rs\` —
\`with_timeout_secs(0)\` is a documented "instant timeout" primitive
that other callers may legitimately want.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:39:11.699057Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:15.687876Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:15.687498Z","close_reason":"Landed in db5bfeaf (fix(mcp_tools): reject timeout_secs=0 in ca.{search,view,status})","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"ft-znu6v","title":"[audit] storage/fts: deferred retention can strand last_indexed_seq past seq reset","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:33:26.451931Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:34:43.704014Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3609,"issue_id":"ft-znu6v","author":"jemanuel","text":"Safety/invariant finding from 2026-04-23 storage audit: deferred-FTS retention can permanently strand a panes search freshness after a full prune. Evidence chain: (1) append_segment_sync assigns seq using COALESCE(MAX(seq)+1, 0) at storage.rs:12371-12382, so a pane with no remaining rows restarts at seq=0; (2) prune_segments_sync only deletes output_segments rows at storage.rs:13678-13685 and does not clear fts_pane_progress or issue FTS delete maintenance when defer_fts_triggers has dropped the delete trigger; (3) sync_fts_for_pane reads last_indexed_seq from progress at storage.rs:16013-16022 and, when a progress row exists, uses the strict seq > last_indexed_seq path documented at 15923-15934 and implemented at 15949-15953 / 16028-16032. Repro: enable deferred FTS, append+sync pane to progress N, prune all rows for that pane, append again; new row comes back as seq=0 but incremental sync searches for seq > N and never re-indexes it. Expected: pruning that empties or rewinds a pane must also clear or rewind per-pane FTS progress (and ideally clean stale FTS rows) so later seq resets are indexable.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:34:43Z"}]}

Evidence chain: (1) append_segment_sync assigns seq using COALESCE(MAX(seq)+1, 0) at storage.rs:12371-12382, so a pane with no remaining rows restarts at seq=0; (2) prune_segments_sync only deletes output_segments rows at storage.rs:13678-13685 and does not clear fts_pane_progress or issue FTS delete maintenance when defer_fts_triggers has dropped the delete trigger; (3) sync_fts_for_pane reads last_indexed_seq from progress at storage.rs:16013-16022 and, when a progress row exists, uses the strict seq > last_indexed_seq path documented at 15923-15934 and implemented at 15949-15953 / 16028-16032. Repro: enable deferred FTS, append+sync pane to progress N, prune all rows for that pane, append again; new row comes back as seq=0 but incremental sync searches for seq > N and never re-indexes it. Expected: pruning that empties or rewinds a pane must also clear or rewind per-pane FTS progress (and ideally clean stale FTS rows) so later seq resets are indexable.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:34:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-ztcsl","title":"mux ClientInfo JSON roundtrip truncates subsecond timestamps","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:00:01.250430Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:13:54.131088Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:13:54.130678Z","close_reason":"Fixed: frankenterm/mux/src/client.rs:42-65 ClientInfo now uses '#[serde(with = ts_milliseconds)]' on both connected_at (line 53) and last_input (line 61) instead of the former ts_seconds.  {"id":"ft-zu8g3","title":"connector_outbound_bridge::OutboundDeduplicator: capacity=0 degrades to 1-slot ghost cache (parallel to ft-bx4le, ft-61kg4)","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_outbound_bridge.rs:596 OutboundDeduplicator::check_and_record with capacity=0 does NOT disable deduplication. The 'if entries.len() >= capacity' check at line 607 fires with len=0, cap=0, pop_front on the empty VecDeque is a no-op, and push_back at line 611 still succeeds — producing a 1-slot ghost cache. Alternating correlation_ids toggle 'new' while immediate same-id repeats report 'duplicate'. Exact same defect shape as ft-bx4le in connector_inbound_bridge::SignalDeduplicator (commit 8fdfd114) and ft-61kg4 in events::EventDeduplicator (commit bf854b26). Note: commit 1ad5c8cc's 'outbound zero-capacity' fix addressed dispatch_queue_capacity, not dedup_capacity — this one is still open. Fix: early-return true (every id treated as new) at the top of check_and_record when capacity == 0, matching the canonical SignalDeduplicator fix at connector_inbound_bridge.rs:351.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:56:01.113371Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:57:09.504002Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:57:09.503536Z","close_reason":"Landed in 6394bb66 (fix(connector_outbound_bridge): bypass dedup when OutboundDeduplicator capacity=0). Early-return true at top of check_and_record when capacity == 0. Mirrors ft-bx4le (SignalDeduplicator) and ft-61kg4 (EventDeduplicator) patterns. Regression test connector_outbound_bridge_dedup_zero_capacity_bypasses_dedup_ft_zu8g3 pins behavior. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-zv3u9","title":"events::Event missing PartialEq; mission_dispatch.rs:542 assert_eq! fails to compile","description":"mission_dispatch.rs:542 uses assert_eq! on crate::events::Event values, but the Event enum in events.rs:146 doesn't derive PartialEq.
